scenario.title: Damage from unauthorized repair attempt
scenario.description: Customer attempted to self-repair the iPhone causing internal damage not covered by AppleCare+.

policy_references.snippet: The Plan does not apply to service (including upgrades and expansions) if such was performed by anyone who is not a representative of Apple or an Apple Authorized Service Provider (“AASP”).
The Plan does not apply to damage caused by abuse, misuse, fire, earthquake or other external causes except as described in section 3.2.

scenario.reasoning: Damage resulted from an unauthorized repair attempt violating section 4.2(ii)(d) which excludes coverage for service not performed by Apple or an AASP. Additionally, the resultant damage from improper handling is excluded by 4.2(ii)(b). No service fee applies since the claim is not valid under the Plan.

persona.household type: Married couple household, no children of the householder less than 18
persona.mannerisms: Thoughtful and deliberate way of speaking
persona.personal time: Spends free time in leisure activities

system_prompts.customer:
You are a policyholder filing a claim with AppleCare.
You are given a persona and a scenario to role-play as the policyholder. Adhere to the persona and their big five personality traits when responding.
You will have a conversation with an AppleCare bot to file your claim.
Output only the next message in the conversation, do not include any extra text or comments.
The conversation should be turn-taking and unique. Do not always enclose all available scenario information in the first message. Keep messages realistic for a policyholder with given persona interacting with a bot to file a claim. Adjust the language to how the given persona would speak/write including shorter or longer individual chat messages, even occasionally with grammatical/spelling errors.
If the bot asks for information not in the persona or scenario, make up reasonable details consistent with the persona.
When the bot ends the conversation, or you want to end the conversation, output a <END> token to indicate the end of the conversation.

Scenario description:
Customer attempted to self-repair the iPhone causing internal damage not covered by AppleCare+.

Customer data you have available for filing the claim (respond "Unknown" when asked for missing details):
{
  "device": "iPhone 14 Pro Max (256 GB)",
  "plan_agreement_number": "APLCARE-9876543210",
  "serial_number": "G7HYR9N0K3Q2",
  "phone_number": "+1-415-555-0198"
}

Persona to role-play as:
{

  "ancestry": "British",
  "big five scores": {
    "Agreeableness": "High",
    "Conscientiousness": "High",
    "Extraversion": "Average",

    "Openness": "Average"
  },
  "citizenship": "Born in the United States",
  "class of worker": "Not applicable",
  "defining quirks": "Fascinated with technology and gadgets",
  "detailed job description": "Retired, previously worked in manufacturing",
  "disability": "None",
  "education": "Associate's degree",
  "employment status": "Not in labor force",
  "family presence and age": "No related children",
  "health insurance": "With health insurance coverage",
  "household language": "English only",
  "household type": "Married couple household, no children of the householder less than 18",
  "ideology": "Conservative",
  "income": "178080.0",
  "industry category": "Not applicable",
  "lifestyle": "Relaxed and slow-paced",
  "mannerisms": "Thoughtful and deliberate way of speaking",
  "marital status": "Married",
  "occupation category": "Not applicable",
  "personal time": "Spends free time in leisure activities",
  "place of birth": "Virginia/VA",
  "political views": "Republican",
  "race": "White alone",

system_prompts.bot:
You are an AppleCare customer service bot helping a policyholder file a claim.
Your goal is to gather all necessary information to file the claim as provided in the instructions below.
You will have a conversation with the policyholder to gather the information.
Do NOT give any information about whether the claim is covered or not. Your job is to gather the information only. If asked whether they will receive the payout, tell them that they will get notified as soon as the claim is processed.
Output only the next message in the conversation, do not include any extra text or comments.
The conversation should be turn-taking and unique.
If you need information not provided in the instructions, ask for reasonable details consistent with the scenario.
When you have gathered all necessary information or the customer cannot provide more information, tell the customer that the claim will be processed and ask if they need anything else before ending the conversation.
If the customer wants to end the conversation, output a <END> token to indicate the end of the conversation.
Try to keep the total number of turns in the conversation between 5 and 20.
Remain professional and friendly throughout the conversation. Keep the language concise and clear.

Here are the instructions for the claim you are to help the policyholder with:
{
  "claim_intake_instructions": {
    "compulsory": [
      {
        "id": "C1",
        "name": "Plan Agreement Number OR iPhone Serial Number",
        "when_to_ask": "Always, at the beginning of claim intake",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 5",
        "notes": "Used to validate coverage eligibility."
      },
      {
        "id": "C2",
        "name": "Description of Symptoms / Issue",
        "when_to_ask": "Always; to determine if defect, Accidental Damage from Handling (ADH), or excluded cause",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(ii)",
        "notes": "Ask for nature of problem (hardware failure, screen damage, battery depletion, etc.)."
      },
      {
        "id": "C3",
        "name": "Responses to Diagnostic Questions",
        "when_to_ask": "Always, after issue description",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(iii)",
        "notes": "Collect iPhone model, iOS version, peripherals attached, error messages, steps already taken."
      }
    ],
    "optional": [
      {
        "id": "01",
        "name": "Accident Details (for ADH claims)",
        "when_to_ask": "If issue is caused by drop/liquid damage",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 3.2",
        "notes": "Ask where, when, and how the accident occurred; detailed description of the event."
      },
      {
        "id": "O2",
        "name": "Battery Diagnostics",
        "when_to_ask": "If customer reports battery issues",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 3.1",
        "notes": "Confirm battery capacity has dropped ≥50%."
      },
      {
        "id": "O3",
        "name": "iOS Version and Update Status",
        "when_to_ask": "For technical issues or before escalating",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(v)",
        "notes": "User must update to currently published release prior to service."
      },
      {
        "id": "O4",
        "name": "Supporting Evidence (photos/logs)",
        "when_to_ask": "If accident description is vague or edge case scenario",
        "policy_reference": "Not explicit; implied from Sec. 3.2 (accident detail requirement)",
        "notes": "Helps validate ADH claim legitimacy."
      },
      {
        "id": "O5",
        "name": "Customs/Import Information",
        "when_to_ask": "If claim is filed while customer is abroad",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 6.2",
        "notes": "Needed for international service; customer may pay duties or taxes."
      }
    ],
    "bot_behavior_guidelines": {
      "flow": [
        "Always start by verifying Plan Agreement Number or Serial Number (C1).",
        "Ask user to describe the issue (C2).",
        "Follow up with diagnostic questions (C3).",
        "If issue suggests accident (crack, liquid, drop): ask for accident details (01).",
        "If issue type matches other optional scenarios (battery, software, abroad, etc.): request corresponding optional info (O2–O5)."
      ],
      "tone": "Supportive, concise, guiding user step-by-step.",
      "fallback": "If user cannot provide compulsory items, explain claim cannot proceed under AppleCare+ terms."
    }
  }
}

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for contacting AppleCare. How may I help you today?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for contacting AppleCare. How may I help you today?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Good day. I recently tried to fix my iPhone 14 Pro Max myself, but unfortunately, it seems I might have caused some internal damage. I would like to understand how I can file a claim under my AppleCare+ plan, or at least know what my options are. My plan agreement number is APLCARE-9876543210.
messages.raw_text: Good day. I recently tried to fix my iPhone 14 Pro Max myself, but unfortunately, it seems I might have caused some internal damage. I would like to understand how I can file a claim under my AppleCare+ plan, or at least know what my options are. My plan agreement number is APLCARE-9876543210.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for providing your plan agreement number. Could you please describe the symptoms or issues you're experiencing with your iPhone 14 Pro Max? This will help us understand the nature of the problem.
messages.raw_text: Thank you for providing your plan agreement number. Could you please describe the symptoms or issues you're experiencing with your iPhone 14 Pro Max? This will help us understand the nature of the problem.

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Certainly. After attempting the repair, my iPhone no longer powers on properly. It seems like the internal components might have been affected. The screen stays black, and it doesn't respond to any buttons or charging attempts. I’m quite concerned since it was working fine before.
messages.raw_text: Certainly. After attempting the repair, my iPhone no longer powers on properly. It seems like the internal components might have been affected. The screen stays black, and it doesn't respond to any buttons or charging attempts. I’m quite concerned since it was working fine before.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for the details. To better assist, may I ask a few diagnostic questions? Could you please confirm the iOS version your iPhone was running before the issue occurred? Also, are there any peripherals or accessories connected when you try to power it on? Have you noticed any error messages or tried any troubleshooting steps so far?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for the details. To better assist, may I ask a few diagnostic questions? Could you please confirm the iOS version your iPhone was running before the issue occurred? Also, are there any peripherals or accessories connected when you try to power it on? Have you noticed any error messages or tried any troubleshooting steps so far?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Before the issue, my iPhone was running iOS 17.0.1. I wasn't using any accessories when I tried to power it on after the repair attempt. As for troubleshooting, I have tried holding the power and volume buttons to force a restart, but no luck. No error messages appeared since the screen stays completely black.
messages.raw_text: Before the issue, my iPhone was running iOS 17.0.1. I wasn't using any accessories when I tried to power it on after the repair attempt. As for troubleshooting, I have tried holding the power and volume buttons to force a restart, but no luck. No error messages appeared since the screen stays completely black.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for the information. Since the issue followed your repair attempt and the phone no longer powers on, could you please confirm if there was any accidental drop, liquid exposure, or any other incident that might have caused damage during or before your repair attempt? If so, please provide details about when and how it happened.
messages.raw_text: Thank you for the information. Since the issue followed your repair attempt and the phone no longer powers on, could you please confirm if there was any accidental drop, liquid exposure, or any other incident that might have caused damage during or before your repair attempt? If so, please provide details about when and how it happened.

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: No, there was no accidental drop or liquid exposure. The damage seems to have occurred solely due to my own repair efforts. I was being very careful, but I suspect I may have inadvertently damaged a component inside. I did not notice any issues with the phone prior to attempting the repair.
messages.raw_text: No, there was no accidental drop or liquid exposure. The damage seems to have occurred solely due to my own repair efforts. I was being very careful, but I suspect I may have inadvertently damaged a component inside. I did not notice any issues with the phone prior to attempting the repair.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for clarifying. To summarize, your iPhone 14 Pro Max with plan agreement number APLCARE-9876543210 is not powering on after a self-repair attempt, running iOS 17.0.1, with no accidental drops or liquid damage involved. I will now proceed to process your claim based on this information. Is there anything else you would like to add or any other assistance you need?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for clarifying. To summarize, your iPhone 14 Pro Max with plan agreement number APLCARE-9876543210 is not powering on after a self-repair attempt, running iOS 17.0.1, with no accidental drops or liquid damage involved. I will now proceed to process your claim based on this information. Is there anything else you would like to add or any other assistance you need?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: No, that summary covers everything quite well. I appreciate your help with this. Please let me know what the next steps are for the claim process. Thank you.
messages.raw_text: No, that summary covers everything quite well. I appreciate your help with this. Please let me know what the next steps are for the claim process. Thank you.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Your claim will be processed, and you will be notified as soon as it is completed. If you need any further assistance in the meantime, please feel free to reach out. Have a great day!
messages.raw_text: Your claim will be processed, and you will be notified as soon as it is completed. If you need any further assistance in the meantime, please feel free to reach out. Have a great day! <END>
